items.q: How does myelination increase the speed of nerve impulse conduction?
items.correct: Action potentials jump between nodes of Ranvier (saltatory conduction), skipping the myelinated sections.
items.misconception: Myelin makes the axon thicker so the signal travels faster through more material.
items.random_wrong: Myelin contains additional ion channels that speed up depolarisation.
items.why: Students think 'more material = faster' rather than understanding that myelin INSULATES so the impulse can leap between gaps (saltatory).

items.q: Where do plants get most of their biomass from?
items.correct: From carbon dioxide in the air — the carbon in plant tissue comes mostly from CO2 fixed during photosynthesis.
items.misconception: Plants get most of their mass from the soil through their roots.
items.random_wrong: Plants gain most of their mass directly from sunlight, which has weight.
items.why: Roots in soil look like the source of food/mass, but actually most plant dry mass is carbon from atmospheric CO2.

items.q: What does negative feedback do in homeostasis?
items.correct: It reverses a change in a controlled variable, bringing it back towards the set point.
items.misconception: Negative feedback makes a process get worse or amplifies the change.
items.random_wrong: Negative feedback stops the body from responding to any internal change.
items.why: The word 'negative' has bad connotations, so students think it makes things worse, missing that it counteracts deviations.

items.q: Burnet's clonal selection theory of immunity dates from:
items.why: It's 1957. “1928” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.
It's Inserting aquaporins in collecting duct. “Closing aquaporins” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.
The real figure is 74 days. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
